file_insights.changes: Added new `Clean` enum variant to `Command` enum with three CLI arguments and placeholder implementation
key_additions: Clean variant with struct fields: dry_run (bool), older_than (Option<u64>), state (Option<String>)
Match arm implementation with println! statements for argument echoing
Documentation comments for command and all arguments

patterns_discovered.description: Using enum variants with named fields for complex CLI command structures, leveraging clap for argument parsing
Using Option<u64> and Option<String> for optional flags like --older-than and --state
Deferring actual implementation while providing echo of arguments for validation, enabling incremental development
Using doc comments (///) on enum variants and field-level #[arg()] documentation for CLI help text

gotchas_discovered.details: Command only echoes arguments; actual file cleanup, age filtering, and dry-run logic not yet implemented
gotchas_discovered.impact: Next subtasks must implement core deletion logic

gotchas_discovered.details: No validation that --state is 'done', 'failed', or 'both'. Free-form String allows invalid values
gotchas_discovered.impact: Should add enum for state or validate in implementation phase

gotchas_discovered.details: Placeholder doesn't branch between dry-run preview and actual deletion
gotchas_discovered.impact: Core implementation must handle conditional execution paths

gotchas_discovered.details: No logic for converting --older-than days into file modification time filtering
gotchas_discovered.impact: Next subtask must implement SystemTime comparisons

discoveries.approach_outcome: SUCCESS - Successfully added Clean variant to Command enum with all three specified CLI arguments (--dry-run, --older-than, --state) using idiomatic Rust/clap patterns. Followed existing codebase conventions for command structure. Placeholder implementation allows validation of CLI parsing before complex logic implementation.

recommendations.recommendation: Validate or restrict --state argument to enum ('done' | 'failed' | 'both') to prevent invalid values
Implement actual file discovery and filtering logic for .cards/done/ and .cards/failed/ directories
Add modification time comparison logic using std::fs::metadata() to filter by --older-than threshold
Implement branching between dry-run (preview with println!) and actual deletion (fs::remove_dir_all)
Add verbose/quiet output flags to control feedback verbosity for both dry-run and actual execution
Add comprehensive testing covering all flag combinations (dry-run with/without filters, state variations)
Consider adding confirmation prompt for non-dry-run deletions unless --force flag is added
